Rationale: Consensus high; private key control over accounts and ether balances emphasized in Homestead launch and EIPs 1–8; absolute ownership via cryptographic signatures with no central seizure mechanism (Homestead launch announcement March 2016; EIPs 1–8).
Gas market and transaction pricing via user-set gas prices and block gas limits allow free market clearing for resource allocation (EIPs 1–8; etherscan.io 2016 data).
Mining rewards and transaction fees incentivize participation and network security (Homestead announcement; bitcointalk.org 2016 threads).
No forced redistribution; voluntary transfers and mining rewards dominant (EIPs 1–8; Homestead announcement).
No entitlements; voluntary community aid exists but not protocol-enforced (bitcointalk.org charity threads 2016).
Chain state continuity maintained post-Frontier with no resets in Homestead transition (Homestead announcement).
Smart contracts and dApps show emerging diversity though limited by gas and language constraints in early 2016 (EIPs 1–8; CoinDesk 2016).
Network operational since 2015 with growing but early-stage persistence indicators (etherscan.io 2016 data).

Contract vulnerabilities exist and gas market allows high fees during spikes but no widespread systemic extraction reported Jan-Jun 2016; design intent caps at moderate positive (EIPs 1–8; ethresear.ch 2016).
Open mining and dApp deployment encourage competition without suppression (Homestead announcement).
Moderate pressure from potential node failures and gas economics but no major existential threats visible in period (Homestead announcement).
Ecosystem actively expands via dApps and tools, though still nascent (CoinDesk 2016).
Open permissionless network with growing developer cohesion around Ethereum vision (Homestead announcement).
Protocol patches via EIPs (e.g., EIP-2,7,8 in Homestead) show lagged but implemented fixes for detected issues (EIPs 1–8).
Basic transparency via public ledger and explorers but no automated detection mechanisms; audited contracts provide limited ceiling at +3 max but insufficient for higher (etherscan.io 2016).
Smart contract architecture enables modular dApps on shared base layer (EIPs 1–8).
Blockchain provides distributed immutable storage for state and transactions (Homestead announcement).
High diversity in community applications and views with no enforcement (ethresear.ch 2016).
Pluralistic with debates on scaling and governance visible in dev discussions (CoinDesk 2016).
Open forums and criticism welcomed without evident banning patterns in 2016 sources (ethresear.ch 2016).
